   219→
   220→### Domain Services

   223→- All services emit OpenTelemetry traces
   224→- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
   225→
   226→### API Routes
   227→- Location: `src/api/routes/`
   228→- Framework: Hono
   229→- All routes have OpenAPI spec
   230→- Use @belt/tools createSchema for validation
   231→
   232→### Workers
   233→- Location: `src/workers/`
   234→- Framework: BullMQ (Redis-backed)
   235→- Central exports: `src/workers/index.ts`
   236→- Use `registerWorker()` to create new workers
   237→- 8 queues: ingestion, tactical_scheduler, strategic_planner, optimizer, manhour_leveling, drift_monitor, notification, report
   238→
   239→**Running Workers:**
   240→```bash
   241→# Start all workers
   242→bun run src/workers/runner.ts
   243→
   244→# Start specific workers only
   245→WORKERS=optimizer,drift_monitor bun run src/workers/runner.ts

   247→# Workers require Redis to be running
   248→docker-compose up -d redis
   249→```
   250→
   251→**Implemented Processors (All 9 production-ready):**
   252→- `optimizer` - Run assignment optimization (C++ OR-Tools or TS fallback)
   253→- `drift_monitor` - Baseline drift analysis
   254→- `ingestion` - CSV mileage file processing
   255→- `maximo_sync` - Bidirectional sync with Maximo (push/pull/full)
   256→- `tactical_scheduler` - PM scheduling for 12-week horizon
   257→- `strategic_planner` - Long-term PM planning (genetic algorithm)
   258→- `manhour_leveling` - Workforce optimization across depots
   259→- `notification` - Email notification delivery
   260→- `report` - Excel/PDF report generation
   261→
   262→### Optimizer IPC
   263→- Protocol: NDJSON over stdin/stdout
   264→- Input: `{ type: "solve", data: {...} }`
   265→- Output: `{ type: "progress" | "solution" | "error", ... }`
   266→

   268→
   269→> **CRITICAL**: See README.md for complete @belt/tools API reference
   270→
   271→**Schema Validation** (replaces Zod):
   272→```typescript
   273→import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';
   274→
   275→const TrainModel = createSchema({
   276→  id: 'string',
   277→  trainNumber: 'string',
   278→  mileageKm: 'number',
   279→  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
   280→});
   281→type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
   282→const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
   283→```
   284→
   285→**State Management** (replaces React hooks + zustand):
   286→```typescript
   287→import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
   288→
   289→const fleetSlice = createSlice({
   290→  id: 'fleet',
   291→  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
   292→  cache: { policy: 'session' },
   293→  reducers: {
   294→    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
   295→  },
   296→});
   297→
   298→const machine = createMachine({
   299→  id: 'mms',
   300→  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
   301→  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
   302→});
   303→
   304→// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
   305→const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
   306→```
   307→
   308→### Forbidden Packages (enforced by .claude/hooks/)
   309→
   310→> These packages are BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh
   311→
   312→| Forbidden | Use Instead |
   313→|-----------|-------------|
   314→| zustand, redux, mobx, xstate | @belt/tools createMachine/createSlice |
   315→| @mui, antd, @patternfly | @blueprintjs/core |
   316→| tailwind | BlueprintJS styles |
   317→| next.js | Vite SPA |
   318→
   319→### React Hook Restrictions (enforced by .agentrc.ts)
   320→
   321→> These React hooks are BLOCKED - use @belt/tools machine instead
   322→
   323→- ❌ useState, useEffect, useReducer, useContext
   324→- ❌ useCallback, useMemo, useLayoutEffect
   325→- ❌ useTransition, useDeferredValue
   326→- ✅ useRef (explicitly allowed)
   327→
   328→## Common Issues
   329→
   330→### Port conflicts
   331→```bash
   332→lsof -i :5432  # Check postgres
   333→lsof -i :6379  # Check redis
   334→```
   335→
   336→### Database connection
   337→```bash
   338→# Reset completely
   339→docker-compose down -v
   340→docker-compose up -d postgres
   341→bun run db:migrate
   342→```
   343→
   344→### Optimizer not found
   345→```bash
   346→# Check binary exists
   347→ls -la mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
   348→
   349→# Rebuild if needed
   350→cd mms-optimizer && cmake --build build --clean-first
   351→```

file.content:
# MMS Operational Guide

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Full stack (dev) - all services via Docker
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up -d

# Hybrid (data in Docker, app local)
docker compose up -d postgres redis
bun install
bun run dev                # Frontend
bun run src/api/server.ts  # Backend (separate terminal)

# Tests
bun test                    # Unit tests
bun test:watch             # Watch mode
bun run test:integration   # Integration (needs Docker)

# Validation (run in order!)
bun run typecheck          # TypeScript strict
bun run lint               # ESLint
bun run build              # Full build
```

## Validation Order (Backpressure)

**IMPORTANT: Run these in order after implementing changes:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path-to-changed-file>

# 2. TypeScript type checking
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (if touching API/DB/services/workers)
PORT=0 bun run test:integration

# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (MANDATORY for UI work)
# See Playwright MCP Verification Workflow below
```

**Port Configuration:**
- Always use `PORT=0` for random port assignment
- Parse actual port from server startup output
- Never hardcode ports in tests

All must pass before committing. 12-year contract demands production-grade quality gates.

## Playwright MCP Verification Workflow

> **MANDATORY** for all UI work (new features AND verification of completed features)

### Step-by-step E2E verification using Playwright MCP:

**1. Start dev server** (in separate terminal):
```bash
PORT=0 bun run dev
```
> **CRITICAL**: Capture the actual port from server output (e.g., "Local: http://localhost:43521")

**2. Navigate with Playwright MCP**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to `http://localhost:<captured-port>`
- **Never hardcode ports** - always use the dynamically assigned port

**3. Capture page state**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to get accessibility tree
- Review snapshot for expected elements

**4. Verify interactions**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_click` for clicks
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_type` for text input
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` for keyboard shortcuts

**5. Validate expected state**:
- Take another snapshot after interactions
- Verify expected changes occurred

**6. Cleanup**:
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_close`

### If Playwright MCP verification fails:
- Task is **NOT** complete
- Document issue in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
- Fix before proceeding

### Key Playwright MCP Tools:
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` | Navigate to URL |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` | Capture accessibility tree |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_click` | Click element by ref |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_type` | Type text into element |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` | Press keyboard key |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot` | Capture visual screenshot |
| `mcp__playwright__browser_close` | Close browser |

### Verification Tasks (Phase 7.8):
- Completed UI features must be verified with Playwright MCP
- New UI features must pass Playwright MCP before being marked complete
- See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 7.8 for full task list

## Build & Run

### Development Environment

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis minio

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Seed demo data (optional)
bun run db:seed

# Start dev server
bun run dev
```

### C++ Optimizer Build

```bash
# Prerequisites (macOS)
brew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt

# Build
cd mms-optimizer
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build

# Binary location: mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
# Environment variable: MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY (default: ./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer)
```

### Docker Full Stack

```bash
# Copy environment example
cp .env.example .env

# Start all services
docker compose up -d

# Start only data services (postgres + redis)
docker compose up -d postgres redis

# Start with dev tools (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
docker compose --profile tools up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f backend
docker compose logs -f worker

# Stop and remove volumes
docker compose down -v

# Services and ports:
# - postgres:5432 (PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB)
# - redis:6379 (Redis 7)
# - backend: random port (check "API_SERVER_PORT=<port>" in logs)
# - frontend: random port (check "Local: http://localhost:<port>" in logs)
# - worker (BullMQ workers, no port)
# - file-watcher (CSV watcher, no port)
# - redis-commander:8081 (tools profile)
# - pgadmin:8082 (tools profile)
#
# Note: Backend/frontend use random ports by default for dev isolation.
# Set PORT=3000 or VITE_PORT=5173 for fixed ports.
```

## Database

### Migrations
```bash
bun run db:migrate              # Run pending
bun run db:migrate:status       # Check status
bun run db:rollback             # Rollback last
```

### Drizzle Studio
```bash
bun run db:studio               # Opens at localhost:4983
```

### Direct Access
```bash
docker exec -it mms-postgres psql -U mms -d mms_dev
```

## Environment Variables

```bash
# Required
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms_dev
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
MINIO_ENDPOINT=localhost:9000
MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=mms
MINIO_SECRET_KEY=mms_secret

# Optional
LOG_LEVEL=debug
MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY=./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
MAXIMO_API_URL=http://maximo.example.com/api
```

## Codebase Patterns

### Domain Services
- Location: `src/services/`
- Pattern: Class with injected dependencies
- All services emit OpenTelemetry traces
- Use Drizzle ORM for database access

### API Routes
- Location: `src/api/routes/`
- Framework: Hono
- All routes have OpenAPI spec
- Use @belt/tools createSchema for validation

### Workers
- Location: `src/workers/`
- Framework: BullMQ (Redis-backed)
- Central exports: `src/workers/index.ts`
- Use `registerWorker()` to create new workers
- 8 queues: ingestion, tactical_scheduler, strategic_planner, optimizer, manhour_leveling, drift_monitor, notification, report

**Running Workers:**
```bash
# Start all workers
bun run src/workers/runner.ts

# Start specific workers only
WORKERS=optimizer,drift_monitor bun run src/workers/runner.ts

# Workers require Redis to be running
docker-compose up -d redis
```

**Implemented Processors (All 9 production-ready):**
- `optimizer` - Run assignment optimization (C++ OR-Tools or TS fallback)
- `drift_monitor` - Baseline drift analysis
- `ingestion` - CSV mileage file processing
- `maximo_sync` - Bidirectional sync with Maximo (push/pull/full)
- `tactical_scheduler` - PM scheduling for 12-week horizon
- `strategic_planner` - Long-term PM planning (genetic algorithm)
- `manhour_leveling` - Workforce optimization across depots
- `notification` - Email notification delivery
- `report` - Excel/PDF report generation

### Optimizer IPC
- Protocol: NDJSON over stdin/stdout
- Input: `{ type: "solve", data: {...} }`
- Output: `{ type: "progress" | "solution" | "error", ... }`

### Validation & State Management (@belt/tools)

> **CRITICAL**: See README.md for complete @belt/tools API reference

**Schema Validation** (replaces Zod):
```typescript
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
```

**State Management** (replaces React hooks + zustand):
```typescript
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

### Forbidden Packages (enforced by .claude/hooks/)

> These packages are BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh

| Forbidden | Use Instead |
|-----------|-------------|
| zustand, redux, mobx, xstate | @belt/tools createMachine/createSlice |
| @mui, antd, @patternfly | @blueprintjs/core |
| tailwind | BlueprintJS styles |
| next.js | Vite SPA |

### React Hook Restrictions (enforced by .agentrc.ts)

> These React hooks are BLOCKED - use @belt/tools machine instead

- ❌ useState, useEffect, useReducer, useContext
- ❌ useCallback, useMemo, useLayoutEffect
- ❌ useTransition, useDeferredValue
- ✅ useRef (explicitly allowed)

## Common Issues

### Port conflicts
```bash
lsof -i :5432  # Check postgres
lsof -i :6379  # Check redis
```

### Database connection
```bash
# Reset completely
docker-compose down -v
docker-compose up -d postgres
bun run db:migrate
```

### Optimizer not found
```bash
# Check binary exists
ls -la mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer

# Rebuild if needed
cd mms-optimizer && cmake --build build --clean-first
```

0a. Study `specs/*` systematically to learn the MMS application specifications. Use subagents for parallel file reads when beneficial. Pay special attention to:
    - 001-executive-summary.md: System purpose, scope, ADRs, risks
    - 002-architecture.md: High-level architecture, component responsibilities
    - 003-database-schema.md: PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB schema
    - 004-domain-services.md: All domain services (Ingestion, Optimizer, PM Scheduler, Strategic Planner, etc.)
    - 005-api.md: REST API specification
    - 006-user-interface.md: Infinite Canvas, deck.gl, BlueprintJS
    - 007-integration.md: Maximo, external systems
    - 008-operations.md: Docker, Kubernetes, monitoring

0b. Study @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md (if present) to understand the plan so far.

0c. Study `src/lib/*` to understand shared utilities & components. Use subagents for parallel exploration when beneficial.

0d. For reference, the application source code is in `src/*`. Key directories:
    - src/services/ - Domain services
    - src/api/ - Hono API routes
    - src/workers/ - BullMQ workers
    - src/optimizer/ - C++ OR-Tools optimizer
    - src/lib/ - Shared utilities

0e. **CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS** (enforced by .claude/hooks/ and .agentrc.ts):
    - **Validation**: Use @belt/tools createSchema/createType
    - **State**: Use @belt/tools machines (NOT React hooks, NOT zustand)
    - **React hooks BLOCKED**: useState, useEffect, useReducer, useContext, useMemo, useCallback
    - **Packages BLOCKED**: zustand, redux, mobx, xstate, @mui, antd, tailwind, next.js
    - **Commits**: No Co-Authored-By lines (clean authorship required)
    - See README.md for @belt/tools API reference

    When planning UI components (Phase 7), ensure:
    - NO React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) - use machine.useMachine()

99999. As soon as there are no build or test errors create a git tag. If there are no git tags start at 0.0.0 and increment patch by 1.

999999. You may add extra logging if required to debug issues. MMS uses OpenTelemetry for observability.

9999999. Keep @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md current with learnings using a subagent — future work depends on this to avoid duplicating efforts. Update especially after finishing your turn.

99999999. When you learn something new about how to run the application, update @AGENTS.md using a subagent but keep it brief. For example:
    - Database connection strings
    - Required environment variables
    - Docker commands for services
    - C++ optimizer build commands

999999999. For any bugs you notice, resolve them or document them in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md using a subagent even if it is unrelated to the current piece of work.

9999999999. Implement functionality completely. Placeholders and stubs waste efforts and time redoing the same work. MMS domain services must be production-ready.

102. **Integration Tests** (MANDATORY when modifying backend):

    Run with random port to avoid conflicts:
    ```bash
    PORT=0 bun run test:integration
    ```

    Integration tests MUST verify:
    - API endpoint contracts (request/response schemas)
    - Service-to-database interactions (real PostgreSQL, not mocked)
    - Worker queue processing (real Redis)
    - WebSocket broadcast delivery
    - Cross-service data flow

103. **E2E Verification via Playwright MCP** (MANDATORY for UI work):

    > **CRITICAL**: UI tasks are NOT complete until Playwright MCP verification passes.
    > This applies to BOTH new features AND verification of completed features (Phase 7.8).

    **For completed tasks being verified:**
    1. Start dev server: `PORT=0 bun run dev` (capture actual port from output)
    2. Navigate: `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` to `http://localhost:<captured-port>`
    3. Snapshot: `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to capture accessibility tree
    4. Verify: Confirm expected elements exist in snapshot
    5. Interact: Test interactions with `mcp__playwright__browser_click`, `browser_type`, `browser_press_key`
    6. Validate: Take final snapshot, confirm expected state changes
    7. Close: `mcp__playwright__browser_close`

    **For new UI implementation:**
    Same workflow MUST pass before marking task as complete.

    **If Playwright MCP verification fails:**
    - Task is NOT complete - do NOT mark as done
    - Document issue in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
    - Fix before proceeding

    **Key Tools:**
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate` - Go to URL
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` - Capture accessibility tree (better than screenshot)
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_click` - Click element by ref
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_type` - Type text
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_press_key` - Keyboard input (test shortcuts)
    - `mcp__playwright__browser_close` - Cleanup

104. **Port Conflict Prevention**:

    ALWAYS use dynamic ports for test isolation:
    ```bash
    # API server
    PORT=0 bun run src/api/server.ts

    # Vite dev server
    VITE_PORT=0 bun run dev

    # Integration tests
    TEST_PORT=0 bun run test:integration
    ```

    Parse actual port from server output before proceeding with tests.

99999999999. When @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md becomes large periodically clean out the items that are completed from the file using a subagent.

999999999999. If you find inconsistencies in the specs/* then use an Opus 4.5 subagent with 'ultrathink' requested to update the specs. MMS has 9 spec documents that must stay consistent.

9999999999999. IMPORTANT: Keep @AGENTS.md operational only — status updates and progress notes belong in @IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. A bloated AGENTS.md pollutes every future loop's context.

99999999999999. MMS-Specific Guidelines:
    - **Validation**: @belt/tools createSchema/createType - see README.md
    - **State**: @belt/tools machines (React hooks BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts)
    - **Commits**: No Co-Authored-By lines (enforced by .agentrc.ts)
    - Database: Use Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB
    - API: Hono framework with OpenAPI spec
    - Workers: BullMQ with Redis
    - Optimizer: C++ with OR-Tools CP-SAT, TypeScript fallback
    - UI: React 18 + deck.gl + BlueprintJS v6 (NO zustand - use @belt/tools)
    - All services must emit OpenTelemetry traces
    - Soft constraints must have explanation traces
    - Audit trail for all schedule changes

999999999999999. Constraint Validation Order (from spec 004):
    1. Hard constraints: Safety, physical limits - NEVER violate
    2. Soft constraints: Compliance windows, preferences - violate with penalty
    3. Material constraints: Check inventory before scheduling PM
    4. Track constraints: Validate track capability for PM type

9999999999999999. Integration Boundaries (from spec 007):
    - Maximo: Receive CM status, send PM work orders
    - Never manage CM work orders (out of scope)
    - Inventory: Consume snapshots only
    - Service demand: Validate minimum availability

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Setup Script for MMS v2.1
# =============================================================================
# This script prepares the MMS project to use the Ralph Wiggum methodology.
#
# Improvements v2.1:
# - All scripts made executable
# - jq installation check
# - Claude CLI settings.json configuration guidance
# - Timeout configuration guidance
#
# Usage:
#   chmod +x setup.sh
#   ./setup.sh
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
log_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

echo ""
echo -e "${BLUE}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}║     Ralph Wiggum Setup for MMS v2.1                              ║${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}║     Original Huntley Method - External Loop, Clean Context       ║${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
echo ""

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"

# =============================================================================
# 1. Check prerequisites
# =============================================================================
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

MISSING_CRITICAL=false
MISSING_OPTIONAL=()

# Claude CLI
if command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Claude CLI found: $(claude --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'version unknown')"
else
    log_error "Claude CLI not found. Install with: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-cli"
    MISSING_CRITICAL=true
fi

# Bun
if command -v bun &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Bun found: $(bun --version)"
else
    log_warn "Bun not found. Install with: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("bun")
fi

# Docker
if command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Docker found: $(docker --version)"
else
    log_warn "Docker not found. Sandbox execution will not be available."
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("docker")
fi

# Git
if git rev-parse --git-dir &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Git repository detected"
else
    log_warn "Not in a git repository. Ralph requires git for state persistence."
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("git-repo")
fi

# jq (required for circuit breaker)
if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "jq found: $(jq --version)"
else
    log_warn "jq not found - circuit breaker features will be limited"
    log_info "Install with: apt install jq (Linux) or brew install jq (macOS)"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("jq")
fi

# envsubst (required for plan-work mode)
if command -v envsubst &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "envsubst found"
else
    log_warn "envsubst not found - plan-work mode may not work"
    log_info "Install with: apt install gettext-base (Linux) or brew install gettext (macOS)"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("envsubst")
fi

# bc (optional, used for floating point math)
if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "bc found"
else
    log_warn "bc not found - using awk fallback for floating point math"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("bc")
fi

if [[ "$MISSING_CRITICAL" == "true" ]]; then
    log_error "Critical dependencies missing. Please install them and re-run setup."
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# 2. Create directory structure
# =============================================================================
log_info "Creating directory structure..."

mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/specs"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/lib"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/services"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/api"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/workers"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/optimizer"

log_success "Directory structure created"

# =============================================================================
# 3. Make ALL scripts executable
# =============================================================================
log_info "Making scripts executable..."

chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/loop.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/monitor.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/setup.sh" 2>/dev/null || true

log_success "All scripts are executable"

# =============================================================================
# 5. Copy specs from project (if they exist)
# =============================================================================
log_info "Checking for existing specs..."

if [[ -d "/mnt/project" ]]; then
    log_info "Found project specs. Copying to specs/..."
    
    # Map existing spec files
    declare -A spec_files=(
        ["MMS_SPEC_001_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md"]="001-executive-summary.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_002_ARCHITECTURE.md"]="002-architecture.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_003_DATABASE_SCHEMA.md"]="003-database-schema.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_004_DOMAIN_SERVICES.md"]="004-domain-services.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_005_API.md"]="005-api.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_006_USER_INTERFACE.md"]="006-user-interface.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_007_INTEGRATION.md"]="007-integration.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_008_OPERATIONS.md"]="008-operations.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_009_DEMO_APPLICATION.md"]="009-demo-application.md"
    )
    
    for src in "${!spec_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "/mnt/project/$src" ]]; then
            dst="${spec_files[$src]}"
            cp "/mnt/project/$src" "$PROJECT_ROOT/specs/$dst"
            log_success "Copied $src → specs/$dst"
        fi
    done
else
    log_warn "No existing project specs found at /mnt/project"
    log_info "You'll need to create specs manually in the specs/ directory"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 6. Create .gitignore entries for Ralph
# =============================================================================
log_info "Updating .gitignore..."

GITIGNORE_ENTRIES="
# Ralph Wiggum
/ralph/.ralph-state/
/tmp/ralph-*

# Docker
docker/ralph-sandbox/postgres_data/
docker/ralph-sandbox/redis_data/
docker/ralph-sandbox/minio_data/
"

if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore" ]]; then
    if ! grep -q "Ralph Wiggum" "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$GITIGNORE_ENTRIES" >> "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore"
        log_success ".gitignore updated"
    else
        log_info ".gitignore already contains Ralph entries"
    fi
else
    echo "$GITIGNORE_ENTRIES" > "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore"
    log_success ".gitignore created"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 7. Claude CLI Settings Configuration (IMPORTANT!)
# =============================================================================
echo ""
echo -e "${YELLOW}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}  IMPORTANT: Claude CLI Timeout Configuration${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo ""
echo "Claude CLI has a default 2-minute timeout on bash commands."
echo "Long-running builds, tests, and optimizer runs will be KILLED!"
echo ""
echo "To configure timeouts, add this to .claude/settings.json in the project root:"
echo ""
echo -e "${CYAN}{"
echo '  "env": {'
echo '    "BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS": "1800000",'
echo '    "BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS": "7200000",'
echo '    "API_TIMEOUT_MS": "600000"'
echo '  }'
echo -e "}${NC}"
echo ""
echo "Alternatively, loop.sh will export these as environment variables."
echo ""

# Create project-level settings file if it doesn't exist
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/.claude"
CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE="$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR/settings.json"

if [[ ! -f "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE" ]]; then
    log_info "Creating project-level Claude settings file..."
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR"
    cat > "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE" << 'EOF'
{
  "env": {
    "BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS": "1800000",
    "BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS": "7200000",
    "API_TIMEOUT_MS": "600000",
    "MCP_TIMEOUT": "30000",
    "CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC": "1"
  }
}
EOF
    log_success "Created .claude/settings.json with timeout configuration"
else
    log_info ".claude/settings.json already exists"
    log_info "Please verify it contains the timeout configuration above"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 8. Summary
# =============================================================================
echo ""
echo -e "${GREEN}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}║     Setup Complete!                                              ║${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
echo ""

if [[ ${#MISSING_OPTIONAL[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Optional dependencies missing:${NC} ${MISSING_OPTIONAL[*]}"
    echo "Some features may be limited. Install them for full functionality."
    echo ""
fi

echo "Next steps:"
echo ""
echo "  1. Verify Claude CLI timeout settings:"
echo "     cat .claude/settings.json"
echo ""
echo "  2. Start with planning mode:"
echo "     cd ralph"
echo "     ./loop.sh plan"
echo ""
echo "  3. Then build:"
echo "     ./loop.sh 50"
echo ""
echo "  For sandbox execution (recommended):"
echo "     cd docker/ralph-sandbox"
echo "     docker-compose up -d"
echo "     docker exec -it ralph-sandbox bash"
echo "     cd /home/ralph/workspace/ralph"
echo "     ./loop.sh"
echo ""
echo "Documentation: ralph/README.md"
echo ""

     2→# =============================================================================
     3→# Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker - Production Version 2.1
     4→# =============================================================================
     5→# Prevents runaway loops and excessive API costs.
     6→#
     7→# Improvements v2.1:
     8→# - bc fallback to awk for systems without bc
     9→# - Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
    10→# - Improved error handling
    11→# - Better jq fallback
    12→#
    13→# Usage (standalone):
    14→#   ./circuit-breaker.sh status    # Show current state
    15→#   ./circuit-breaker.sh reset     # Reset all state
    16→#   ./circuit-breaker.sh check     # Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)
    17→#
    18→# Usage (sourced by loop.sh):
    19→#   source circuit-breaker.sh
    20→#   cb_init
    21→#   cb_check_circuit || exit 1
    22→#   cb_record_iteration
    23→#   cb_record_error "error message"
    24→#
    25→# The circuit opens when:
    26→# - No progress (git commits) for N iterations
    27→# - Same error repeated N times
    28→# - Estimated cost exceeds limit
    29→# =============================================================================
    30→
    31→# Configuration (can be overridden via environment)
    32→CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD=${CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD:-9999999} # This value is high by design, DO NOT CHANGE.
    33→CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD=${CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD:-5}
    34→CB_MAX_COST_USD=${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999} # This value is high by design, DO NOT CHANGE.
    35→CB_STATE_DIR="${CB_STATE_DIR:-/tmp/ralph-circuit-breaker}"
    36→CB_STATE_FILE="${CB_STATE_DIR}/state.json"

    39→# Input: ~5k tokens ($0.015/1k = $0.075)
    40→# Output: ~10k tokens ($0.075/1k = $0.75)
    41→# Subagents: ~$0.10 average
    42→# Total: ~$0.40-0.50 per iteration
    43→COST_PER_ITERATION=${COST_PER_ITERATION:-0.45}

    45→# Colors
    46→RED='\033[0;31m'
    47→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    48→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    49→CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    50→NC='\033[0m'
    51→
    52→# =============================================================================
    53→# UTILITY FUNCTIONS
    54→# =============================================================================
    55→
    56→# Safe floating point addition (bc or awk fallback)
    57→safe_add() {
    58→    local a="$1"
    59→    local b="$2"
    60→    if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
    61→        echo "$a + $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    62→    else
    63→        awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    64→    fi
    65→}
    66→
    67→# Safe floating point comparison (bc or awk fallback)
    68→safe_gte() {
    69→    local a="$1"
    70→    local b="$2"

    72→        [[ $(echo "$a >= $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -eq 1 ]]
    73→    else
    74→        awk "BEGIN {exit !($a >= $b)}"
    75→    fi
    76→}
    77→
    78→# Cross-platform date from timestamp
    79→date_from_timestamp() {
    80→    local timestamp="$1"
    81→    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    82→        # GNU date (Linux)
    83→        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    84→    else
    85→        # BSD date (macOS)
    86→        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    87→    fi
    88→}
    89→
    90→# Cross-platform ISO date
    91→iso_date() {
    92→    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    93→        # GNU date
    94→        date -Iseconds 2>/dev/null || date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    95→    else
    96→        # BSD date (macOS)
    97→        date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    98→    fi
    99→}
   100→
   101→# Check if jq is available
   102→has_jq() {
   103→    command -v jq &> /dev/null
   104→}
   105→
   106→# =============================================================================
   107→# CORE FUNCTIONS
   108→# =============================================================================
   109→
   110→cb_init() {
   111→    mkdir -p "$CB_STATE_DIR"
   112→    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
   113→        local start_time
   114→        start_time=$(date +%s)
   115→        
   116→        if has_jq; then
   117→            echo '{
   118→                "iterations": 0,
   119→                "last_commit": "",
   120→                "no_progress_count": 0,
   121→                "errors": [],
   122→                "estimated_cost": 0,
   123→                "start_time": '"$start_time"',
   124→                "session_id": "'$$'"
   125→            }' | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
   126→        else
   127→            # Fallback without jq
   128→            cat > "$CB_STATE_FILE" << EOF
   129→{
   130→    "iterations": 0,
   131→    "last_commit": "",
   132→    "no_progress_count": 0,
   133→    "errors": [],
   134→    "estimated_cost": 0,
   135→    "start_time": $start_time,
   136→    "session_id": "$$"
   137→}
   138→EOF
   139→        fi
   140→    fi
   141→}
   142→
   143→cb_get_state() {
   144→    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
   145→        cb_init
   146→    fi
   147→    cat "$CB_STATE_FILE"
   148→}
   149→
   150→cb_update_state() {
   151→    local state="$1"
   152→    if has_jq; then
   153→        echo "$state" | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
   154→    else
   155→        echo "$state" > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
   156→    fi
   157→}
   158→
   159→cb_record_iteration() {
   160→    if ! has_jq; then
   161→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit breaker recording limited"
   162→        return 0
   163→    fi
   164→    
   165→    local state
   166→    state=$(cb_get_state)
   167→    local current_commit
   168→    current_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "none")
   169→    local last_commit
   170→    last_commit=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.last_commit')
   171→    local iterations
   172→    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
   173→    local no_progress
   174→    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
   175→    local cost
   176→    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
   177→    
   178→    iterations=$((iterations + 1))
   179→    cost=$(safe_add "$cost" "$COST_PER_ITERATION")
   180→    
   181→    if [[ "$current_commit" == "$last_commit" ]]; then
   182→        no_progress=$((no_progress + 1))
   183→    else
   184→        no_progress=0
   185→    fi
   186→    
   187→    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
   188→        --arg commit "$current_commit" \
   189→        --argjson iter "$iterations" \
   190→        --argjson np "$no_progress" \
   191→        --arg cost "$cost" \
   192→        '.last_commit = $commit | .iterations = $iter | .no_progress_count = $np | .estimated_cost = ($cost | tonumber)')
   193→    
   194→    cb_update_state "$state"
   195→}
   196→
   197→cb_record_error() {
   198→    local error_msg="$1"
   199→    
   200→    if ! has_jq; then
   201→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - error not recorded"
   202→        return 0
   203→    fi
   204→    
   205→    local state
   206→    state=$(cb_get_state)
   207→    local timestamp
   208→    timestamp=$(iso_date)
   209→    
   210→    # Create error object with timestamp
   211→    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
   212→        --arg err "$error_msg" \
   213→        --arg ts "$timestamp" \
   214→        '.errors += [{"message": $err, "timestamp": $ts}] | .errors = .errors[-10:]')
   215→    
   216→    cb_update_state "$state"
   217→}
   218→
   219→cb_check_circuit() {
   220→    if ! has_jq; then
   221→        # Without jq, we can't properly check the circuit
   222→        # Fail open (allow execution) but warn
   223→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit check skipped" >&2
   224→        return 0
   225→    fi
   226→    
   227→    local state
   228→    state=$(cb_get_state)
   229→    local no_progress
   230→    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
   231→    local cost
   232→    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
   233→    local iterations
   234→    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
   235→    
   236→    # Check no progress
   237→    if [[ $no_progress -ge $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD ]]; then
   238→        echo "No progress for $no_progress iterations (threshold: $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD)"
   239→        return 1
   240→    fi
   241→    
   242→    # Check cost limit
   243→    if safe_gte "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"; then
   244→        printf "Estimated cost \$%.2f exceeds limit \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
   245→        return 1
   246→    fi
   247→    
   248→    # Check repeated errors (same error message 5+ times in last 10)
   249→    local error_messages
   250→    error_messages=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '[.errors[].message] | group_by(.) | map({message: .[0], count: length}) | sort_by(-.count) | .[0]')
   251→    local top_error_count
   252→    top_error_count=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.count // 0')
   253→    local top_error_msg
   254→    top_error_msg=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.message // "none"')
   255→    
   256→    if [[ $top_error_count -ge $CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD ]]; then
   257→        echo "Same error repeated $top_error_count times: $top_error_msg"
   258→        return 1
   259→    fi
   260→    
   261→    return 0
   262→}
   263→
   264→cb_status() {
   265→    if ! has_jq; then
   266→        echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
   267→        echo "  jq not installed - detailed status unavailable"
   268→        echo "  State file: $CB_STATE_FILE"
   269→        [[ -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]] && echo "  Raw state: $(cat "$CB_STATE_FILE" | head -c 200)..."
   270→        return 0
   271→    fi
   272→    
   273→    local state
   274→    state=$(cb_get_state)
   275→    local iterations
   276→    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
   277→    local no_progress
   278→    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
   279→    local cost
   280→    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
   281→    local error_count
   282→    error_count=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors | length')
   283→    local start_time
   284→    start_time=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.start_time')
   285→    local elapsed
   286→    elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
   287→    
   288→    echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
   289→    echo "  Session start:   $(date_from_timestamp "$start_time")"
   290→    echo "  Elapsed:         ${elapsed}s"
   291→    echo "  Iterations:      $iterations"
   292→    echo "  No progress:     $no_progress / $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD"
   293→    printf "  Estimated cost:  \$%.2f / \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
   294→    echo "  Recent errors:   $error_count"
   295→    
   296→    if cb_check_circuit > /dev/null 2>&1; then
   297→        echo -e "  Status:          ${GREEN}CLOSED (OK)${NC}"
   298→    else
   299→        echo -e "  Status:          ${RED}OPEN (BLOCKED)${NC}"
   300→        echo ""
   301→        echo -e "${YELLOW}Reason:${NC}"
   302→        cb_check_circuit 2>&1 | sed 's/^/  /'
   303→    fi
   304→}
   305→
   306→cb_reset() {
   307→    rm -f "$CB_STATE_FILE"
   308→    cb_init
   309→    echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit breaker reset${NC}"
   310→}
   311→
   312→cb_show_errors() {
   313→    if ! has_jq; then
   314→        echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
   315→        echo "  jq not installed - cannot show errors"
   316→        return 0
   317→    fi
   318→    
   319→    local state
   320→    state=$(cb_get_state)
   321→    echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
   322→    echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors[] | "  [\(.timestamp)] \(.message)"' 2>/dev/null || echo "  (none)"
   323→}
   324→
   325→# =============================================================================
   326→# MAIN (when executed directly)
   327→# =============================================================================
   328→
   329→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
   330→    case "${1:-status}" in
   331→        init)
   332→            cb_init
   333→            echo "Circuit breaker initialized"
   334→            ;;
   335→        record)
   336→            cb_record_iteration
   337→            echo "Iteration recorded"
   338→            ;;
   339→        error)
   340→            if [[ -z "$2" ]]; then
   341→                echo "Usage: $0 error \"error message\""
   342→                exit 1
   343→            fi
   344→            cb_record_error "$2"
   345→            echo "Error recorded"
   346→            ;;
   347→        check)
   348→            if cb_check_circuit; then
   349→                echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit CLOSED (OK to proceed)${NC}"
   350→                exit 0
   351→            else
   352→                echo -e "${RED}Circuit OPEN (blocked)${NC}"
   353→                exit 1
   354→            fi
   355→            ;;
   356→        status)
   357→            cb_status
   358→            ;;
   359→        errors)
   360→            cb_show_errors
   361→            ;;
   362→        reset)
   363→            cb_reset
   364→            ;;
   365→        *)
   366→            echo "Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker v2.1"
   367→            echo ""
   368→            echo "Usage: $0 [command]"
   369→            echo ""
   370→            echo "Commands:"
   371→            echo "  status    Show current state (default)"
   372→            echo "  check     Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)"
   373→            echo "  reset     Reset all state"
   374→            echo "  errors    Show recent errors"
   375→            echo "  init      Initialize state file"
   376→            echo "  record    Record an iteration"
   377→            echo "  error MSG Record an error message"
   378→            echo ""
   379→            echo "Environment Variables:"
   380→            echo "  CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD  Iterations without commit (default: 10)"
   381→            echo "  CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD   Repeated error count (default: 5)"
   382→            echo "  CB_MAX_COST_USD           Cost limit in USD (default: 9999999 - unlimited for 24h+ runs)"
   383→            echo "  COST_PER_ITERATION        Est. cost per iteration (default: 0.45)"
   384→            exit 1
   385→            ;;
   386→    esac
   387→fi
   388→

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker - Production Version 2.1
# =============================================================================
# Prevents runaway loops and excessive API costs.
#
# Improvements v2.1:
# - bc fallback to awk for systems without bc
# - Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
# - Improved error handling
# - Better jq fallback
#
# Usage (standalone):
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh status    # Show current state
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh reset     # Reset all state
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh check     # Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)
#
# Usage (sourced by loop.sh):
#   source circuit-breaker.sh
#   cb_init
#   cb_check_circuit || exit 1
#   cb_record_iteration
#   cb_record_error "error message"
#
# The circuit opens when:
# - No progress (git commits) for N iterations
# - Same error repeated N times
# - Estimated cost exceeds limit
# =============================================================================

# Configuration (can be overridden via environment)
CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD=${CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD:-9999999} # This value is high by design, DO NOT CHANGE.
CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD=${CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD:-5}
CB_MAX_COST_USD=${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999} # This value is high by design, DO NOT CHANGE.
CB_STATE_DIR="${CB_STATE_DIR:-/tmp/ralph-circuit-breaker}"
CB_STATE_FILE="${CB_STATE_DIR}/state.json"

# Cost estimate per iteration (Opus model)
# Input: ~5k tokens ($0.015/1k = $0.075)
# Output: ~10k tokens ($0.075/1k = $0.75)
# Subagents: ~$0.10 average
# Total: ~$0.40-0.50 per iteration
COST_PER_ITERATION=${COST_PER_ITERATION:-0.45}

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# =============================================================================
# UTILITY FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

# Safe floating point addition (bc or awk fallback)
safe_add() {
    local a="$1"
    local b="$2"
    if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
        echo "$a + $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    else
        awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    fi
}

# Safe floating point comparison (bc or awk fallback)
safe_gte() {
    local a="$1"
    local b="$2"
    if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
        [[ $(echo "$a >= $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -eq 1 ]]
    else
        awk "BEGIN {exit !($a >= $b)}"
    fi
}

# Cross-platform date from timestamp
date_from_timestamp() {
    local timestamp="$1"
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date (Linux)
        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    fi
}

# Cross-platform ISO date
iso_date() {
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date
        date -Iseconds 2>/dev/null || date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    fi
}

# Check if jq is available
has_jq() {
    command -v jq &> /dev/null
}

# =============================================================================
# CORE FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

cb_init() {
    mkdir -p "$CB_STATE_DIR"
    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
        local start_time
        start_time=$(date +%s)
        
        if has_jq; then
            echo '{
                "iterations": 0,
                "last_commit": "",
                "no_progress_count": 0,
                "errors": [],
                "estimated_cost": 0,
                "start_time": '"$start_time"',
                "session_id": "'$$'"
            }' | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
        else
            # Fallback without jq
            cat > "$CB_STATE_FILE" << EOF
{
    "iterations": 0,
    "last_commit": "",
    "no_progress_count": 0,
    "errors": [],
    "estimated_cost": 0,
    "start_time": $start_time,
    "session_id": "$$"
}
EOF
        fi
    fi
}

cb_get_state() {
    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
        cb_init
    fi
    cat "$CB_STATE_FILE"
}

cb_update_state() {
    local state="$1"
    if has_jq; then
        echo "$state" | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    else
        echo "$state" > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    fi
}

cb_record_iteration() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit breaker recording limited"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local current_commit
    current_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "none")
    local last_commit
    last_commit=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.last_commit')
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    
    iterations=$((iterations + 1))
    cost=$(safe_add "$cost" "$COST_PER_ITERATION")
    
    if [[ "$current_commit" == "$last_commit" ]]; then
        no_progress=$((no_progress + 1))
    else
        no_progress=0
    fi
    
    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
        --arg commit "$current_commit" \
        --argjson iter "$iterations" \
        --argjson np "$no_progress" \
        --arg cost "$cost" \
        '.last_commit = $commit | .iterations = $iter | .no_progress_count = $np | .estimated_cost = ($cost | tonumber)')
    
    cb_update_state "$state"
}

cb_record_error() {
    local error_msg="$1"
    
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - error not recorded"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(iso_date)
    
    # Create error object with timestamp
    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
        --arg err "$error_msg" \
        --arg ts "$timestamp" \
        '.errors += [{"message": $err, "timestamp": $ts}] | .errors = .errors[-10:]')
    
    cb_update_state "$state"
}

cb_check_circuit() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        # Without jq, we can't properly check the circuit
        # Fail open (allow execution) but warn
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit check skipped" >&2
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    
    # Check no progress
    if [[ $no_progress -ge $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD ]]; then
        echo "No progress for $no_progress iterations (threshold: $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cost limit
    if safe_gte "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"; then
        printf "Estimated cost \$%.2f exceeds limit \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check repeated errors (same error message 5+ times in last 10)
    local error_messages
    error_messages=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '[.errors[].message] | group_by(.) | map({message: .[0], count: length}) | sort_by(-.count) | .[0]')
    local top_error_count
    top_error_count=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.count // 0')
    local top_error_msg
    top_error_msg=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.message // "none"')
    
    if [[ $top_error_count -ge $CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD ]]; then
        echo "Same error repeated $top_error_count times: $top_error_msg"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

cb_status() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
        echo "  jq not installed - detailed status unavailable"
        echo "  State file: $CB_STATE_FILE"
        [[ -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]] && echo "  Raw state: $(cat "$CB_STATE_FILE" | head -c 200)..."
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    local error_count
    error_count=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors | length')
    local start_time
    start_time=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.start_time')
    local elapsed
    elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
    
    echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
    echo "  Session start:   $(date_from_timestamp "$start_time")"
    echo "  Elapsed:         ${elapsed}s"
    echo "  Iterations:      $iterations"
    echo "  No progress:     $no_progress / $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD"
    printf "  Estimated cost:  \$%.2f / \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
    echo "  Recent errors:   $error_count"
    
    if cb_check_circuit > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo -e "  Status:          ${GREEN}CLOSED (OK)${NC}"
    else
        echo -e "  Status:          ${RED}OPEN (BLOCKED)${NC}"
        echo ""
        echo -e "${YELLOW}Reason:${NC}"
        cb_check_circuit 2>&1 | sed 's/^/  /'
    fi
}

cb_reset() {
    rm -f "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    cb_init
    echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit breaker reset${NC}"
}

cb_show_errors() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
        echo "  jq not installed - cannot show errors"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
    echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors[] | "  [\(.timestamp)] \(.message)"' 2>/dev/null || echo "  (none)"
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN (when executed directly)
# =============================================================================

if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    case "${1:-status}" in
        init)
            cb_init
            echo "Circuit breaker initialized"
            ;;
        record)
            cb_record_iteration
            echo "Iteration recorded"
            ;;
        error)
            if [[ -z "$2" ]]; then
                echo "Usage: $0 error \"error message\""
                exit 1
            fi
            cb_record_error "$2"
            echo "Error recorded"
            ;;
        check)
            if cb_check_circuit; then
                echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit CLOSED (OK to proceed)${NC}"
                exit 0
            else
                echo -e "${RED}Circuit OPEN (blocked)${NC}"
                exit 1
            fi
            ;;
        status)
            cb_status
            ;;
        errors)
            cb_show_errors
            ;;
        reset)
            cb_reset
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker v2.1"
            echo ""
            echo "Usage: $0 [command]"
            echo ""
            echo "Commands:"
            echo "  status    Show current state (default)"
            echo "  check     Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)"
            echo "  reset     Reset all state"
            echo "  errors    Show recent errors"
            echo "  init      Initialize state file"
            echo "  record    Record an iteration"
            echo "  error MSG Record an error message"
            echo ""
            echo "Environment Variables:"
            echo "  CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD  Iterations without commit (default: 10)"
            echo "  CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD   Repeated error count (default: 5)"
            echo "  CB_MAX_COST_USD           Cost limit in USD (default: 9999999 - unlimited for 24h+ runs)"
            echo "  COST_PER_ITERATION        Est. cost per iteration (default: 0.45)"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
fi

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# =============================================================================
     3→# Ralph Wiggum Loop - Production Hardened Version 2.1
     4→# =============================================================================
     5→# Based on Geoffrey Huntley's ORIGINAL method (external loop, clean context)
     6→# 
     7→# CRITICAL FIXES v2.1:
     8→# 1. Claude CLI timeout configuration (CRITICAL - prevents 2-min kill)
     9→# 2. API timeout configuration
    10→# 3. Retry logic for transient failures
    11→# 4. Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
    12→# 5. bc dependency fallback
    13→# 6. Fixed variable scope issues
    14→# 7. Improved error handling
    15→# =============================================================================
    16→
    17→set -euo pipefail
    18→
    19→# Colors for output
    20→RED='\033[0;31m'
    21→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    22→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    23→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    24→CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    25→NC='\033[0m'
    26→
    27→# =============================================================================
    28→# CONFIGURATION
    29→# =============================================================================
    30→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    31→PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
    32→
    33→# Source circuit breaker
    34→if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" ]]; then
    35→    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh"
    36→    CB_ENABLED=true
    37→else
    38→    CB_ENABLED=false
    39→    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} circuit-breaker.sh not found - running without protection"
    40→fi
    41→
    42→# Ralph files
    43→PROMPT_BUILD="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_build.md"
    44→PROMPT_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan.md"
    45→PROMPT_PLAN_WORK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan_work.md"
    46→AGENTS_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/AGENTS.md"
    47→IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
    48→
    49→# Output formatter (optional, for human-readable output)
    50→FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/scripts/format-output.sh"
    51→PRETTY_OUTPUT=${PRETTY_OUTPUT:-true}
    52→
    53→# Default model
    54→DEFAULT_MODEL="opus"
    55→
    56→# Rate limiting
    57→MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR=${MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR:-9999999} # This value is high by design, DO NOT CHANGE.
    58→CALLS_FILE="/tmp/ralph-calls-$(date +%Y%m%d%H)"
    59→
    60→# Output capture
    61→OUTPUT_DIR="/tmp/ralph-outputs-$$"
    62→mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"
    63→
    64→# =============================================================================
    65→# TIMEOUT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
    66→# =============================================================================
    67→# Claude CLI has a default 2-minute timeout on bash commands.
    68→# This will kill long-running builds, tests, and optimizer runs!
    69→# Configure these environment variables to extend timeouts.
    70→#
    71→# Reference: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/5615
    72→# =============================================================================
    73→
    74→# Bash command timeout (ms) - default 30 minutes
    75→export BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS:-1800000}
    76→# Maximum bash timeout (ms) - default 2 hours
    77→export BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS:-7200000}
    78→# API request timeout (ms) - default 10 minutes
    79→export API_TIMEOUT_MS=${API_TIMEOUT_MS:-600000}
    80→# MCP server startup timeout (ms) - default 30 seconds
    81→export MCP_TIMEOUT=${MCP_TIMEOUT:-30000}
    82→# Disable non-essential traffic (improves stability)
    83→export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=${CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC:-1}
    84→
    85→# Retry configuration for Claude CLI calls
    86→MAX_RETRIES=${MAX_RETRIES:-3}
    87→RETRY_DELAY=${RETRY_DELAY:-5}
    88→
    89→# Completion signals to detect
    90→# NOTE: Includes all signals from request.sh templates (FIX, DOCS, TESTS, FEATURE)
    91→COMPLETION_SIGNALS=(
    92→    "ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE"
    93→    "PLAN_COMPLETE"
    94→    "EXIT_SIGNAL"
    95→    "<promise>DONE</promise>"
    96→    "<promise>COMPLETE</promise>"
    97→    "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"
    98→    "<promise>PLAN_COMPLETE</promise>"
    99→    "<promise>FIX_COMPLETE</promise>"
   100→    "<promise>DOCS_COMPLETE</promise>"
   101→    "<promise>TESTS_COMPLETE</promise>"
   102→    "<promise>FEATURE_COMPLETE</promise>"
   103→    "<promise>RALPH_BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE</promise>"
   104→    "<promise>BLOCKED</promise>"
   105→)
   106→
   107→# =============================================================================
   108→# HELPER FUNCTIONS
   109→# =============================================================================
   110→
   111→log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }

   113→log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
